For maps constructed through the standard path, this holds up. Overall, the code in map.c is reasonably well-written with appropriate null checks and assertions protecting most pointer dereferences. - Allocations (calloc/malloc) are always checked

- The cleanup path handles errors properly
- Pointer chains are verified before dereferencing
